name: Physical Intelligence π0
short_description: A generalist robot foundation model trained across 8 platforms and multiple dexterous tasks, using a vision-language-action architecture with flow matching.
long_description: Announced in October 2024, π0 (pi-zero) is the first robot policy to demonstrate that a single model can be trained across 8 distinct robot platforms and perform a wide range of dexterous manipulation tasks — laundry folding, box assembly, table bussing, grocery bagging, and more — without per-task specialization. Built on a 3-billion-parameter vision-language model backbone extended with continuous action outputs via flow matching (a diffusion-model variant), it outputs motor commands at up to 50 Hz. Its generality across platforms and tasks marks a structural shift from the single-task specialist paradigm that dominated robot learning, establishing the concept of a robot foundation model as a practical architectural commitment rather than a research aspiration.
ai_note: VLA foundation model: a 3B-parameter vision-language model augmented with continuous action outputs via flow matching, fine-tuned for dexterous manipulation across multiple robot platforms.

images.caption: Physical Intelligence π0 robot folding laundry, demonstrating dexterous manipulation across a generalist policy.
images.alt_text: A robot arm manipulating a piece of clothing on a table as part of Physical Intelligence's pi-zero generalist policy demonstration.

sources.title: π0: Physical Intelligence's Generalist Robot Policy
sources.access_notes: Official announcement blog post, published October 31, 2024. Describes the model architecture, robot platforms, and demonstrated tasks.

sources.title: π₀: A Vision-Language-Action Flow Model for General Robot Control
sources.access_notes: Peer-reviewed paper (RSS 2025). Authors include Kevin Black, Noah Brown, Danny Driess, Chelsea Finn, Karol Hausman, Brian Ichter, Sergey Levine, and others from Physical Intelligence. Describes the flow-matching architecture and evaluation across dexterous tasks.

interactions.title: Fold a shirt from a laundry hamper
interactions.description: π0 picks an unfolded shirt from a pile, manipulates it through multiple intermediate steps, and folds it into a flat square — a task Physical Intelligence notes no prior robot system had demonstrated at this level of complexity.

interactions.title: Bus a table, sorting dishes from trash
interactions.description: The robot clears a restaurant-style table by identifying dishes, cups, and trash and sorting them into the appropriate bins — achieving 0.971 on the easy bussing benchmark and 0.875 on the hard variant in evaluations against OpenVLA and Octo baselines.

interactions.title: Assemble a cardboard box from flat panels
interactions.description: Given flat cardboard pieces, π0 folds and assembles them into a box — a multi-step dexterous task requiring spatial reasoning and sequenced force application.

interactions.title: Bag a jumble of grocery items
interactions.description: The robot stows a variety of grocery items into a bag, handling different shapes and weights — one of the suite of tasks spanning household dexterity in the π0 demonstrations.

interactions.title: Transfer the trained policy to a new robot with minimal fine-tuning
interactions.description: Because π0 is trained across 8 distinct robot morphologies, a team could potentially adapt the pre-trained weights to a new arm or mobile manipulator by fine-tuning on a small task-specific dataset rather than retraining from scratch — the core architectural bet of the foundation model approach.
